# Presentation — Render, Stitch, Export

The AMD Hackathon (Track 1) video presentation, built with
[Manim CE](https://www.manim.community/) + [manim-slides](https://manim-slides.eertmans.be/).
Slides live in [`slides.py`](slides.py); each scene is one slide.

**Deck order:** `Title` → `Objective` → `Architecture` → `Iteration` → `Results` → `Closing`

**Deliverables** (both committed, both regenerated by the commands below):

| File | What it is |
|---|---|
| [`final.mp4`](final.mp4) | The submission video — 1920×1080, 60 fps, ~62 s, no audio |
| [`deck.pdf`](deck.pdf) | 6-page 16:9 handout, one page per slide (the final frame of each) |

All commands below are **PowerShell**, run from the repo root (`C:\Me\Projects\amd-hack`).

---

## Prerequisites

- **Python 3.13** at `C:\Users\Wayne\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python313\`
  with `manim` (0.19.1), `manim-slides` (5.5.2) and `img2pdf` (PDF export only).
  > ⚠️ Manim is **not** in the project's `agent/.venv` — that venv is only for the
  > agent. Use the global Python above to render.
- **LaTeX** (MiKTeX) on PATH — the slides typeset all text with `Tex`.
- **ffmpeg is not required.** Manim 0.19 encodes video through PyAV (bundled),
  so rendering works with nothing extra installed.

> ⚠️ **Do not call bare `manim` or `manim-slides`.** Both resolve to Anaconda's copies
> (`C:\Users\Wayne\anaconda3\Scripts\`), which are broken — `manim --version` alone
> tracebacks, and `manim-slides` raises `numpy.dtype size changed`. Always go through the
> Python 3.13 interpreter as a module.

Set this once per shell; every command below uses it:

```powershell
$py = "C:\Users\Wayne\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python313\python.exe"
```

Install the PDF dependency once:

```powershell
& $py -m pip install img2pdf
```

---

## 1. Build the video (`final.mp4`)

`manim` writes **one MP4 per scene**, so a flat video is a two-step job: render every
scene, then stitch them with [`build_video.py`](build_video.py).

```powershell
& $py -m manim -qh --media_dir presentation\media presentation\slides.py Title Objective Architecture Iteration Results Closing
& $py presentation\build_video.py
start presentation\final.mp4
```

- `-qh` = 1920×1080 @ 60 fps. Drafts: `-ql` (480p, fast) · `-qm` (720p). `-qk` = 4K.
- Per-scene output lands in `presentation\media\videos\slides\1080p60\`.
- `build_video.py` with **no arguments** stitches the whole deck in order; pass scene
  names to stitch a subset. It remuxes through PyAV (no standalone ffmpeg needed).

> ⚠️ `manim-slides convert` **cannot** produce an MP4 — it only registers HTML
> (reveal.js), PDF and PPTX writers. Its own `concatenate_video_files` helper also
> crashes on Windows (it hands ffmpeg's concat demuxer a backslash path and raises a
> spurious `FileNotFoundError`). Hence the local script.

---

## 2. Build the PDF (`deck.pdf`)

The PDF is one page per slide, each page being that slide's **final frame** — so it must
run **after** step 1 (it reads `slides\*.json` and the clips under `slides\files\`, which
only exist once the scenes have been rendered to video).

```powershell
& $py -m manim_slides convert Title Objective Architecture Iteration Results Closing presentation\deck.pdf
start presentation\deck.pdf
```

The same command produces a self-contained reveal.js slideshow if you swap the extension:

```powershell
& $py -m manim_slides convert Title Objective Architecture Iteration Results Closing presentation\deck.html
```

---

## 3. Quick preview (static image, fastest)

Renders just a scene's last frame to a PNG — no video encoding. Best way to check layout.

```powershell
& $py -m manim -s -r 1920,1080 --media_dir presentation\media presentation\slides.py Results
start presentation\media\images\slides\Results_ManimCE_v0.19.1.png
```

> Two quirks of `-s` mode, both harmless: a `Failed to merge basenames` error prints at the
> very end (the manim-slides teardown expects video files; the PNG is already written), and
> because that error aborts the process, **only the first scene renders** if you pass several.
> Preview one scene at a time.

---

## 4. Present live (interactive)

Drive the deck with the keyboard (arrows / space to advance, `q` to quit) instead of
playing a flat video:

```powershell
& $py -m manim_slides present Title Objective Architecture Iteration Results Closing
```

---

## Editing the deck

**Pacing** is controlled from the top of `slides.py`:

- `PACE` (default `1.4`) multiplies *every* animation's `run_time` — raise it to slow the
  whole deck down uniformly.
- `BEAT` is the short pause between animation groups within a slide.
- Each scene ends with an explicit `self.wait(...)` sized to how much there is to read;
  the dense slides (`Iteration`, `Results`) hold longest.

**Layout** follows one grammar, worth preserving:

- `CONTENT_W` (12.2) is the deck's measure — the same width as the footer hairline. Every
  full-width element is held to it, so nothing overhangs the rule beneath it.
- Two-column slides (`Objective`, `Results`) stack the headline on two lines and use a
  left-aligned, non-flanked eyebrow.
- Full-width slides (`Architecture`, `Iteration`) run the headline on one line and centre a
  flanked eyebrow, which buys vertical room for the diagram below.
- Everything boxed goes through `panel()`; a coloured border means "this one matters".

**LaTeX traps** (all text is `Tex`, not `Text`):

- `tracked()` letterspaces by inserting `\ \ ` between glyphs, so it **mangles escaped
  characters** — never pass it `\%` or `$\cdot$`. Track each plain segment, then join.
- A long `Tex` string wraps at LaTeX's own measure, usually in an ugly place. Split multi-line
  body copy into a `VGroup` of one `Tex` per line.
- `%`, `&` and `_` must be escaped (`\%`, `\&`, `\_`), including inside `\texttt{}`.

---

## Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Cause / Fix |
|---|---|
| `Failed to merge basenames` after `-s` | Harmless manim-slides teardown in image mode; the PNG is still written. It aborts the run, so preview one scene at a time. |
| `'str' object has no attribute 'to_hex'` after a video render | The scene's `background_color` must be a `ManimColor(...)`, not a raw hex string. (Already handled in `slides.py`.) |
| `numpy.dtype size changed` on `manim-slides` | The `manim-slides.exe` on PATH is Anaconda's. Use `& $py -m manim_slides ...`. |
| Bare `manim` tracebacks on any command | Same cause: `manim.exe` on PATH is Anaconda's. Use `& $py -m manim ...`. |
| `No module named 'img2pdf'` on PDF export | `& $py -m pip install img2pdf` (see Prerequisites). |
| `Cannot merge an empty list of files` on PDF export | You converted before rendering. Run step 1 first — `convert` needs `slides\*.json`. |
| `latex` / `dvisvgm` errors | Ensure MiKTeX is on PATH; the first run may prompt to install missing packages. |
| Standalone `ffmpeg`/`ffprobe` fails (exit 53) | The conda-cache copy is broken — and not needed. Manim/manim-slides use PyAV. |
| Stale frames after editing | Delete `presentation\media\` and re-render (or add `--disable_caching`). |
| `LaggedStartMap(FadeIn, group, shift=...)` does nothing | `shift` isn't forwarded per-animation. Build `LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(m, shift=...) for m in group])`. |
